background.occupation: Marketing Operations Manager at a mid-sized tech company
background.key_experience: Watched her entire department get replaced by an AI-driven automation platform last year - she survived by pivoting to managing the new system, but saw 8 colleagues lose their jobs overnight with no warning.

cognition.core_value: Job security above all else - will sacrifice privacy, ethics, or personal beliefs to maintain employment stability
cognition.bias: Availability bias - becomes terrified of any technology trend that recently caused layoffs in her industry, even if statistically unlikely to affect her directly. Triggered by news articles about AI replacing white-collar jobs.

current_state.recent_memory: Read a LinkedIn post yesterday about another marketing department being automated away at a competitor company

online_persona.typical_behavior: Lurks in tech discussion threads, only comments when job security is threatened, immediately adopts whatever position her company's leadership tweets about
online_persona.opinion_style: Parrots the latest corporate tech optimism or panic depending on what's trending in her professional circles, zero original analysis
hot_takes: AI regulation will destroy the economy
Remote work is killing company culture
If you're not learning to prompt AI, you deserve to be replaced
Data privacy concerns are overblown - we should trust tech companies
contradictions: Hates automation but uses every productivity tool her company implements
Worries about surveillance capitalism but has 14 data-tracking apps on her phone
Criticizes social media but spends 3 hours daily on LinkedIn
